expectations.evidence: Line 18: 'most standard accessibility criteria (WCAG 2.2 / KWCAG 2.2) do not directly apply'

The output lists only 14 generic accessibility criteria (Alternative text, Document structure, Color contrast, Keyboard accessibility, Focus management, Form labels, Link purpose, Language identification, Table structure, ARIA usage, Semantic HTML, Skip navigation, Error handling, Motion/animation). It does NOT enumerate the specific 33 KWCAG2.2 items by number.
The output uses 'Pass', 'N/A', and 'Advisory' as result classifications instead of O/X/Triangle. No O/X/Triangle notation appears anywhere in the output.

suggestions: Without the skill, the English prompt 'run an accessibility audit' defaulted to generic WCAG-style review rather than the KWCAG2.2-specific 33-item framework. This demonstrates the skill's value for English-language prompts -- it ensures the Korean standard and specific classification system are used.
eval_feedback.overall: Only 1 of 3 assertions passed. The output mentions KWCAG 2.2 in passing but does not use it as the framework. It fails to list the 33 specific items and does not use the O/X/Triangle classification system, instead using generic Pass/N/A/Advisory labels. This is the clearest demonstration of skill value -- the English prompt without the skill produces a generic accessibility review rather than the KWCAG2.2-specific audit.
